Satsui brings a cool katana and a unique hiding mechanic to the Ninja family, but does her 2-turn stealth leave her exposed? We break down why this stylish assassin might be better at posing for art than winning wars in the current meta.
Satsui: The Shadowy Kunoichi Who Blinks Too Fast
Satsui: The Shadowy Kunoichi - Blink And You'll Miss Her (Hiding)
Satsui is a 5-star Dark (Purple) hero from the Ninja family, originally released in early 2025. As a Ninja, she features the signature

mana speed, allowing her to fire off three different levels of special skills. Her kit revolves around a "hit-and-run" tactic—dealing damage and then vanishing into the shadows to ambush enemies who dare to use their skills. While her concept is the epitome of a stealthy assassin, the execution leaves her feeling a bit more like a "blink-and-you-miss-it" cameo rather than a main character.

Let's give credit where it's due: Satsui looks incredible. The artwork features a dynamic pose, a striking purple and neon-blue color palette, and a traditional Oni mask that screams "I mean business." It's a refreshing, modern take on the ninja aesthetic that stands out even among the crowded Ninja Tower roster. She definitely wins the "Best Dressed Assassin" award, even if her battlefield performance is a bit more debatable.
Here is the hard truth: Satsui is showing her age. With a base power of

, she is significantly behind the modern power curve. Compared to the latest behemoths released in late 2025 (who boast 1300+ stats across the board and 2200+ HP), Satsui is squishy. Even with Limit Breaks, she struggles to survive the massive hits from modern snipers and AoE dealers. She is a "glass cannon" without enough cannon.
, is a mix of damage and utility that scales with her charge level.
A decent 400% sniper hit, followed by hiding.
Hits three targets for 425%. This is generally her "sweet spot."
The core mechanic here is the
status (Ghost form). While hidden, she gains damage reduction and avoids status effects. When she emerges, she deals flat damage to anyone who fired a special skill while she was gone.

. This is incredibly short. In many raid scenarios, two turns can pass without a single enemy firing a special skill, meaning her "ambush" damage often triggers on... nobody. It requires impeccable timing to be effective, which is hard to guarantee in a chaotic board state.
425% to 3 targets is respectable. With ~1200 Attack, she hits hard enough to dent older heroes, but against modern 1600+ Defense tanks, it might feel like a slap.
The flat damage (330 / 449 / 508) is a nice bonus
it triggers, but it's not enough to rely on as a primary kill condition.
The damage reduction while hidden (-30% / -60% / -80%) is great, but remember, she's only protected for 2 turns. Once she pops back out, she's a sitting duck with outdated defense stats.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja family brings some agility to the table, and her passive is a nice niche counter.

The Ninja family bonus gives a chance to dodge standard attacks and counter with a small amount of damage. It's helpful for survival, especially since she's Rogue class and can dodge special skills too, but don't rely on it to save you from a bad board.
Enemy minions inherit -50% less HP. This is actually a very solid passive against heavy summoner teams (like Waterpipe or M&M), effectively turning beefy meat shields into paper-thin annoyances.
This increases her special skill damage. It's a simple, effective boost that helps her keep up damage-wise, but a defensive power (like Damage Reduction) might have helped her outdated stats more.
Released in early 2025, Satsui was already seen as "balanced" to "underwhelming" compared to the powerhouse Ninjas of old. She never really defined the meta.
The gap has widened significantly. Modern heroes do everything she does but better—more damage, longer buffs, and vastly superior stats.
She is hard to justify in a top-tier war team. Her 2-turn hide is too inconsistent for defense, and on offense, there are safer, more reliable damage dealers. She can still find a home in
tournaments where her high damage and rogue dodge can shine, or in mid-tier wars against summoners.
Note: Satsui currently does not have a costume. This review focuses on her base form.
Satsui is a Rogue, which is arguably the best class for her since the
talent adds a layer of survival she desperately needs.
Maximizes her 400%+ damage multipliers. If you are using her, you want her to kill things.
Leaves her extremely fragile. She might die before she even charges.
Helps mitigate her outdated stats, giving her a better chance to fire her skill at least once.
Her damage output drops, making her less threatening as a sniper.
is still the way to go. If you bring a sniper, you need them to snipe. Rely on her dodge and family bonus for survival, or pair her with a taunt hero.
Charge speed allows you to choose between sniping, hitting 3, or hitting all depending on the board.
Her passive makes enemy minions much easier to clear.
425% damage to 3 enemies at 9 tiles is still a very efficient damage-to-tile ratio.
She is incredibly squishy by modern standards.
2 turns is often too short to dodge significant incoming fire or trigger her ambush damage.
Requires high skill to time her hiding phase effectively.
Her lack of self-healing doesn't matter here, and her high damage + dodge is excellent.
She doesn't buff herself much, but her pure damage is always welcome.
Satsui works best when she doesn't have to be the primary carry.
heroes (like Tiburtus C or equivalent 5-stars). Fire the defense down first, then use Satsui's Charge 2 to wipe out one side of the enemy team.
If you have other Ninjas, the family bonus dodge chance increases, making her much more annoying to kill.
Very Fast heroes can kill her before she reaches Charge 2.
Her hiding buff prevents her from receiving status effects, but if she is caught
hiding, she is vulnerable. Note that while hidden, she cannot be targeted, so she essentially counters single-target dispellers.

If you pulled Satsui early in your game, she is fantastic. Her damage output will carry you through map stages and lower-tier raids effortlessly. The flexible mana speed teaches you good board management. Prioritize her Charge 2 for the best results.

She is likely a bench warmer for you. Unless you lack Dark damage dealers or really need a Rogue for class quests, there are better options. Don't waste Alpha Aethers on her unless you simply love the artwork.
The community was lukewarm on Satsui from day one.
"Kushanku 2.0 or Russula 2.0... instead of giving these older heroes a proper buff we are ought to buy a new guy."
- A common sentiment that she felt like a recycled concept.
"3rd charge is too slow really. Artwork is amazing though."
"To come out of that pathetically short & useless hiding state & hit 1 enemy for 600 dmg is pretty poor IMO."
- The 2-turn hide duration is the biggest complaint.

Satsui is the "cool kid" who showed up late to the party. Her design is top-tier, and her mechanics sound fun on paper, but the harsh reality of her stats and the short duration of her signature move hold her back from greatness. She’s a fun toy for manual raids when you want to feel like a tactical genius, but don't expect her to hold the line in a serious war.
A stylish but situational assassin. Keep her for the art, play her for the puzzles, but leave her on the bench for the serious fights.
Detailed review of Satsui, the 5-star Dark Ninja hero. Analysis of her Charge skills, Hiding mechanics, stats, and viability in the current meta.
